entities.name: Direct Travel, Inc.
entities.description: The Travel Management Company (TMC) and service provider party to the Travel Services Agreement, providing Duty of Care and Travel Risk Management services to Client.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel, Inc. ("Direct Travel" or "TMC")

entities.name: Client
entities.description: The organization party to the Travel Services Agreement receiving Duty of Care and Travel Risk Management services from Direct Travel.

source_anchor.source_text: Client

entities.name: Travel Services Agreement
entities.description: The foundational contractual agreement between Client and Direct Travel into which Exhibit D is incorporated, establishing the terms and conditions for all services.

source_anchor.source_text: Travel Services Agreement (the "Agreement")

entities.name: EXHIBIT D — DUTY OF CARE & TRAVEL RISK MANAGEMENT SERVICES
entities.description: The exhibit document incorporated into the Travel Services Agreement that defines Duty of Care and Travel Risk Management services, including severity level classifications, service level objectives, roles and responsibilities, data handling requirements, and service limitations.

source_anchor.source_text: EXHIBIT D — DUTY OF CARE & TRAVEL RISK MANAGEMENT SERVICES. This Exhibit is incorporated into the Travel Services Agreement

relationships.description: The Travel Services Agreement incorporates Exhibit D, which defines Duty of Care and Travel Risk Management services.
Client is a party to the Travel Services Agreement, receiving Duty of Care and Travel Risk Management services.
Direct Travel, Inc. is a party to the Travel Services Agreement as the TMC provider of Duty of Care and Travel Risk Management services.

entities.name: direct_travel_inc
entities.description: Travel Management Company serving as service provider under the Travel Services Agreement, providing Duty of Care and Travel Risk Management services to Client.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel will provide to support Client's travel risk management

entities.name: client
entities.description: Organization party to the Travel Services Agreement receiving Duty of Care and Travel Risk Management services, and retaining primary responsibility for employer duty of care obligations.

source_anchor.source_text: This Exhibit defines (a) Duty of Care services Direct Travel will provide to support Client's travel risk management

entities.name: travel_risk_management
entities.description: Travel risk management (TRM) program supported by Direct Travel to assist Client in meeting employer duty of care obligations, encompassing severity level classifications, service level objectives, roles, responsibilities, and data handling requirements.

source_anchor.source_text: Client's travel risk management ("TRM") program

entities.name: Duty of Care Services
entities.description: Services Direct Travel provides to support Client's travel risk management program, including severity level classifications, service level objectives, and incident response capabilities.

source_anchor.source_text: Duty of Care services Direct Travel will provide to support Client's travel risk management

entities.name: Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970
entities.description: United States federal law establishing employer duty of care obligations, specifically Section 5(a)(1) General Duty Clause (OSHA General Duty Clause), which Direct Travel services assist Client in meeting.

source_anchor.source_text: OSHA General Duty Clause, Section 5(a)(1) of the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970

entities.name: Health and Safety at Work Act 1974
entities.description: United Kingdom legislation establishing employer duty of care obligations under which Direct Travel services assist Client in achieving compliance.

source_anchor.source_text: Health and Safety at Work Act 1974

entities.name: Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999
entities.description: United Kingdom statutory instrument establishing specific employer health and safety management requirements and duty of care obligations.

source_anchor.source_text: Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999

entities.name: Corporate Manslaughter and Corporate Homicide Act 2007
entities.description: United Kingdom legislation establishing corporate liability for deaths caused by gross breach of duty of care, relevant to Client's employer obligations.

source_anchor.source_text: Corporate Manslaughter and Corporate Homicide Act 2007

entities.name: Framework Directive 89/391/EEC
entities.description: European Union framework directive on employer duty of care and health and safety at work establishing obligations that Direct Travel services support Client in meeting.

source_anchor.source_text: Framework Directive 89/391/EEC

entities.name: ISO 31030:2021
entities.description: International standard for travel risk management derived from ISO 31000, establishing industry best practices that inform Direct Travel's duty of care services.

source_anchor.source_text: ISO 31030:2021 (Travel Risk Management), derived from ISO 31000

entities.name: ISO 31000
entities.description: International standard for risk management providing the foundational framework from which ISO 31030:2021 is derived.

source_anchor.source_text: ISO 31030:2021 (Travel Risk Management), derived from ISO 31000

entities.name: Client Primary Responsibility for Duty of Care
entities.description: Client retains primary responsibility for its duty of care obligations as an employer. Direct Travel provides services to enable and support Client's TRM program but does not assume the employer's duty of care obligation.

source_anchor.source_text: Client retains primary responsibility for its duty of care obligations as an employer. Direct Travel provides services to enable and support Client's TRM program and does not assume the employer's duty of care.

entities.conditionality: Unconditional and perpetual — Client always retains primary responsibility regardless of services provided by Direct Travel

entities.name: Commercially Reasonable Efforts Standard
entities.description: Direct Travel will perform services using commercially reasonable efforts consistent with generally accepted practices in the travel management industry. The standard is one of reasonableness, not perfection.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel will perform services using commercially reasonable efforts consistent with generally accepted practices in the travel management industry. The standard is one of reasonableness, not perfection.

entities.conditionality: Applies to all Direct Travel services under this Exhibit; limited to commercially reasonable efforts, not an absolute or perfection standard

relationships.description: Direct Travel provides Duty of Care services to support Client's travel risk management program, as stated: 'Duty of Care services Direct Travel will provide to support Client's travel risk management program'
Direct Travel provides travel risk management services and support as defined in the Exhibit to enable Client's TRM program
Client engages Direct Travel as the service provider for Duty of Care and travel risk management services under the Travel Services Agreement
Client retains primary responsibility for its duty of care obligations as an employer, as stated: 'Client retains primary responsibility for its duty of care obligations as an employer'
Direct Travel is assigned the obligation to perform services using commercially reasonable efforts consistent with generally accepted practices in the travel management industry
Client's primary duty of care responsibility is supported by Direct Travel's Duty of Care services, which 'enable and support Client's TRM program' in meeting employer duty of care obligations

The TRM program is designed to assist Client in meeting employer duty of care obligations, as stated: 'Services herein assist Client in meeting employer obligations under applicable duty of care frameworks'
Direct Travel's performance standard of commercially reasonable efforts applies to the delivery of Duty of Care services
The commercially reasonable efforts standard governs how Direct Travel executes the TRM program, consistent with generally accepted industry practices

entities.name: Severity Level 1
entities.description: Advisory classification for minor disruptions; informational alerts only. Examples include localized weather warnings and minor transport delays.

source_anchor.source_text: Level 1 | Advisory | Minor disruption; informational alert only | Localized weather warning, minor transport delay

entities.name: Severity Level 2
entities.description: Monitor classification for moderate disruptions; targeted alerts issued and optional welfare checks conducted. Examples include regional transport strikes and elevated security alerts.

source_anchor.source_text: Level 2 | Monitor | Moderate disruption; targeted alerts; optional welfare check | Regional transport strike, elevated security alert

entities.name: Severity Level 3
entities.description: Action Required classification for significant disruptions requiring proactive outreach. Examples include major weather events in traveler areas, airport closures, and disease outbreaks.

source_anchor.source_text: Level 3 | Action Required | Significant disruption; proactive outreach required | Major weather event in Traveler area, airport closure, disease outbreak

entities.name: Severity Level 4
entities.description: Crisis classification for immediate life safety risks requiring urgent outreach and crisis escalation. Examples include terrorist attacks, catastrophic natural disasters, and pandemic border closures.

source_anchor.source_text: Level 4 | Crisis | Immediate life safety risk; urgent outreach and crisis escalation | Terrorist attack, catastrophic natural disaster, pandemic border closures

entities.client_escalation_time_target: within 30 min; Crisis Bridge within 60 min

entities.name: SAFE
entities.description: Traveler response indicating not in danger and no assistance needed. TMC action is to log status with no further outreach.

source_anchor.source_text: SAFE | Not in danger; no assistance needed | Log status; no further action

entities.name: NEED ASSISTANCE
entities.description: Traveler response indicating requirement for support. TMC must attempt live contact to provide assistance.

source_anchor.source_text: NEED ASSISTANCE | Requires support | Attempt live contact within 15 min

entities.name: NOT IN AREA
entities.description: Traveler response indicating they are not near the affected area. TMC action is to remove them from active outreach.

source_anchor.source_text: NOT IN AREA | Not near affected area | Remove from active outreach

entities.name: No Response
entities.description: Traveler has not replied within the outreach window. TMC action is to escalate to Client contacts.

source_anchor.source_text: No Response | No reply within outreach window | Escalate to Client contacts

entities.name: Risk Intelligence Platform
entities.description: The platform used by Direct Travel for severity determination and risk intelligence monitoring; currently implemented as Crisis24 Horizon.

source_anchor.source_text: Severity determination is made by Direct Travel using commercially reasonable judgment informed by the Risk Intelligence Platform (currently Crisis24 Horizon).

entities.name: Welfare Check Outreach
entities.description: Multi-step procedure for determining traveler welfare status during incidents. Default contact priority: SMS, email, push notification, voice call. Triggered by incidents of varying severity; timing and cadence determined by severity level. Traveler responses (SAFE, NEED ASSISTANCE, NOT IN AREA, No Response) determine subsequent TMC actions and escalation.

source_anchor.source_text: Welfare checks follow default priority: (1) SMS, (2) email, (3) push notification, (4) voice call. Travelers confirm status using: ... Response | Meaning | TMC Action

entities.trigger_condition: Incident classification at Level 2 or higher requiring traveler welfare determination
entities.time_constraint: Varies by severity level: Level 1 (within 4 hours), Level 2 (within 2 hours), Level 3 (within 60 minutes), Level 4 (within 30 minutes)

entities.name: Direct Travel, Inc.
entities.description: The Travel Management Company (TMC) providing Duty of Care and Travel Risk Management services to Client. Responsible for severity determination using commercially reasonable judgment informed by risk intelligence.

source_anchor.source_text: Severity determination is made by Direct Travel using commercially reasonable judgment informed by the Risk Intelligence Platform

entities.name: Client
entities.description: The organization receiving Duty of Care and Travel Risk Management services from Direct Travel. Receives incident escalations and status updates at specified service level objectives.

source_anchor.source_text: Client Escalation | ... | Escalation roster within 60 minutes | ... No Response | ... | Escalate to Client contacts

relationships.description: Direct Travel uses and operates the Risk Intelligence Platform (Crisis24 Horizon) to inform severity determination for travel incidents, applying commercially reasonable judgment.

entities.name: direct_travel_inc
entities.description: Direct Travel, Inc., serving as the data processor for Traveler Data under Client's documented instructions.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel acts as a **data processor**, processing Traveler Data solely on Client's documented instructions.

entities.name: client
entities.description: The Client, acting as data controller for Traveler Data, responsible for establishing lawful basis, conducting required assessments, and authorizing data uses including incident-triggered sharing.

source_anchor.source_text: Client is the **data controller** for Traveler Data.

entities.name: data_processing_agreement
entities.description: Data Processing Agreement (DPA), incorporated by reference, governing Direct Travel's role as data processor and regulating lawful processing of Traveler Data on Client's instructions.

source_anchor.source_text: Where required, a Data Processing Agreement ("**DPA**") is incorporated by reference.

entities.name: Full Legal Name
entities.description: Full legal name of the traveler, required for traveler identification and supplier matching. If missing, prevents confirmation of traveler identity.

source_anchor.source_text: **Full legal name** | Traveler identification, supplier matching | Cannot confirm identity
source_anchor.source_section: SEC-04, 3.3 Minimum Required Traveler Profile Data

entities.name: Mobile Phone Number
entities.description: SMS-capable mobile phone number of the traveler, required as the primary welfare check communication channel. If missing, SMS outreach is unavailable.

source_anchor.source_text: **Mobile phone number** (SMS-capable) | Primary welfare check channel | SMS outreach unavailable
source_anchor.source_section: SEC-04, 3.3 Minimum Required Traveler Profile Data

entities.name: Email Address
entities.description: Email address of the traveler, required as secondary communication channel for alerts and notifications. If missing, email alerts are unavailable.

source_anchor.source_text: **Email address** | Secondary communication channel | Email alerts unavailable
source_anchor.source_section: SEC-04, 3.3 Minimum Required Traveler Profile Data

entities.name: Emergency Contact
entities.description: Emergency contact information (name, relationship, phone number) for the traveler, required for next-of-kin notification during incidents. If missing, Client must provide this information during incident response.

source_anchor.source_text: **Emergency contact** (name, relationship, phone) | Next-of-kin notification | Client must provide during incident
source_anchor.source_section: SEC-04, 3.3 Minimum Required Traveler Profile Data

entities.name: Passport Details
entities.description: Passport details for international travel, required for documentation verification. If missing, travel documents cannot be verified.

source_anchor.source_text: **Passport details** (international travel) | Documentation verification | Cannot verify travel documents
source_anchor.source_section: SEC-04, 3.3 Minimum Required Traveler Profile Data

entities.name: GDPR
entities.description: General Data Protection Regulation, specifically Article 6(1)(f) (Legitimate Interest), which provides the lawful basis for processing Traveler Data to protect health and safety of travelers.

source_anchor.source_text: Processing is supported by Client's **legitimate interest** in protecting health and safety of travelers under Article 6(1)(f) GDPR and equivalent laws.

entities.name: Specialist Providers
entities.description: Specialist Providers (security, medical, evacuation services) authorized in the Statement of Work, who may receive minimum necessary Traveler Data during incident response.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel may share **minimum necessary** Traveler Data with: (a) Client's escalation contacts; (b) travel suppliers for rebooking; (c) Specialist Providers (security, medical, evacuation) authorized in the SOW

entities.name: Travel Suppliers
entities.description: Travel suppliers (airlines, hotels, ground transportation, etc.) who may receive minimum necessary Traveler Data for rebooking and service recovery during incidents.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel may share **minimum necessary** Traveler Data with: (a) Client's escalation contacts; (b) travel suppliers for rebooking

entities.name: Government Authorities
entities.description: Government authorities who may receive Traveler Data when required by law or necessary to protect life safety.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel may share **minimum necessary** Traveler Data with: (d) government authorities where required by law or to protect life safety.

relationships.description: Direct Travel acts as data processor under the Data Processing Agreement, governing its processing of Traveler Data on Client's documented instructions.
Client is the data controller party to the Data Processing Agreement, responsible for establishing lawful basis and controlling Direct Travel's processing of Traveler Data.
Data Processing Agreement complies with GDPR Article 6(1)(f), which establishes the lawful basis (legitimate interest in protecting traveler health and safety) for processing Traveler Data.

Client engages Specialist Providers (security, medical, evacuation services) authorized in the Statement of Work to provide incident response services and receive minimum necessary Traveler Data during incidents.
Client engages travel suppliers for rebooking and service recovery during incidents; Direct Travel may share minimum necessary Traveler Data with travel suppliers for these rebooking purposes.
